title: Two Cars In Every Garage And Three Eyes

jokes.text: I feel tranquility outweighs catching fish and use nothing
jokes.setup: Reporter asks what bait he uses, expecting a normal fishing answer
jokes.punchline: Bart's pretentious philosophical reasoning for using no bait at all
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart pretentiousness played deadpan - character-specific absurd logic that's both philosophical and completely impractical

jokes.text: Bart Simpson. Who the hell are you?
jokes.setup: Polite introduction from adult reporter
jokes.punchline: Bart's characteristically rude response to an elder
jokes.explanation: Pure Bart character moment - perfectly economical delivery of his rebellious attitude

jokes.text: Well, this is my day, and we do, sir
jokes.setup: Reporter's complaint about kids not respecting elders 'in his day'
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact generational rebuttal
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that's both logical and defiant - perfect Bart response with quotable structure

jokes.text: One. Two. Three.
jokes.setup: Bart catches a fish and celebrates
jokes.punchline: Counting reveals the fish has three eyes - the mutant fish that drives the episode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual reveal that sets up the entire episode - maximum economy with perfect delayed realization

jokes.text: Getting Washington and Lincoln's birthdays as separate holidays
jokes.setup: Asked what he'd do better as governor than Mary Bailey
jokes.punchline: Moe's petty, personal grievance about President's Day
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific complaint that feels authentically petty and working-class

jokes.text: Keep the mutants coming! / I'll mutant you
jokes.setup: Worker joking about nuclear plant creating mutants
jokes.punchline: Moe's threat using 'mutant' as a verb
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that works through Moe's aggressive delivery style

jokes.text: Thanks for taking all the fancies, guys! Why can't I ever get here on time?
jokes.setup: Homer arrives late to work
jokes.punchline: His passive-aggressive complaint about getting stuck with plain donuts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - self-aware but still blaming external factors for his own lateness

jokes.text: Hold me, Smithers
jokes.setup: Government inspectors arriving
jokes.punchline: Burns' childlike fear manifesting in need for physical comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Burns' power and his infantile vulnerability - economical character revelation

jokes.text: Gum used to seal crack in cooling tower. / I'm as shocked as you
jokes.setup: Inspector finds dangerous safety violation
jokes.punchline: Burns' obviously insincere shock at his own plant's condition
jokes.explanation: Good irony playing on Burns' transparent dishonesty about his own plant's violations

jokes.text: Yeah, well, that's always been like that
jokes.setup: Inspector finds plutonium rod used as paperweight
jokes.punchline: Burns' casual dismissal of nuclear safety violation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns response - treating deadly negligence as minor inconvenience with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Just resting my eyes! / Well done. A rested employee is a vigilant employee
jokes.setup: Homer caught sleeping on the job
jokes.punchline: Burns' spin turning Homer's laziness into a positive
jokes.explanation: Great character interplay - Homer's transparent excuse met with Burns' corporate doublespeak

jokes.text: Some careless person has left thousands of dollars just lying here on my table
jokes.setup: Burns trying to bribe the inspector
jokes.punchline: His transparently obvious attempt to make bribery seem accidental
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - Burns' hilariously transparent attempt at plausible deniability for bribery

jokes.text: The money and a stupid man are still here
jokes.setup: Burns and Smithers return expecting bribery to have worked
jokes.punchline: Inspector's blunt assessment calling Burns stupid to his face
jokes.explanation: Sharp deadpan insult that deflates Burns' ego perfectly - economical and satisfying

jokes.text: Take it! Take it, you poor schmo!
jokes.setup: Inspector refusing the bribe
jokes.punchline: Burns' desperate, insulting attempt to force bribery
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' desperation and condescension when his usual tactics fail

jokes.text: Approximately $56 million. / 56 million?! / Don't hit me, sir
jokes.setup: Burns asks cost to fix plant violations
jokes.punchline: Smithers anticipating violence from the shocking amount
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Smithers knows Burns well enough to expect violent reaction to bad news

jokes.text: Oh, that I had the strength to
jokes.setup: Burns threatening to hit Smithers
jokes.punchline: His admission that he's too physically weak to follow through
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - his evil intentions undermined by his frail, ancient body

jokes.text: Just resting my eyes!
jokes.setup: Homer alone at night in empty plant
jokes.punchline: Using same excuse even when nobody's around to fool
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's ingrained habits - he uses the excuse even when alone, revealing it's almost unconscious

jokes.text: Echo!
jokes.setup: Homer alone in empty nuclear plant
jokes.punchline: Testing the echo like a kid in an empty building
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer childlike behavior - treating nuclear plant like playground with maximum economy

jokes.text: You and I are a dying breed
jokes.setup: Burns finding Homer 'working late'
jokes.punchline: Praising Homer's work ethic when he was actually just goofing around
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Burns completely misreading Homer's presence as dedication rather than goofing off

jokes.text: Cushy!
jokes.setup: Burns invites Homer into his luxurious car
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple, enthusiastic reaction to luxury
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer moment - simple appreciation for comfort with perfect economy of expression

jokes.text: Well, nobody's perfect
jokes.setup: Burns complaining about contaminating the planet
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of environmental destruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement - treating global environmental damage as minor flaw with perfect Homer logic

jokes.text: Don't get me wrong. You're an honest man. I just meant you could afford to run for governor
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting Burns could run for office
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward backpedaling revealing he thinks Burns is dishonest but rich
jokes.explanation: Good Homer social awkwardness - accidentally insulting Burns while trying to be helpful

jokes.text: If you were governor, you could decide what's safe
jokes.setup: Homer encouraging Burns to run for governor
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting corruption of safety standards for personal benefit
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy - Homer innocently suggesting authoritarian corruption as practical solution

jokes.text: To create a new and better world. / If it's on the way, could you drop me at my house?
jokes.setup: Burns' grandiose declaration about their destination
jokes.punchline: Homer's mundane, practical request
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Burns' megalomaniacal ambitions and Homer's simple domestic needs

jokes.text: A political discussion at our table. I feel like a Kennedy!
jokes.setup: Family arguing about politics at dinner
jokes.punchline: Homer's misguided pride at having political discourse
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - aspirational comparison to political dynasty over basic family argument

jokes.text: Why are my teeth showing like that? / Because you're smiling! / Excellent!
jokes.setup: Burns looking at photo of himself smiling
jokes.punchline: Burns doesn't recognize his own smile, treats it like learned behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Burns so evil he doesn't recognize basic human expressions of happiness

jokes.text: This is the kind of trickery I'm paying you for
jokes.setup: Burns learning to smile
jokes.punchline: Calling basic human emotion 'trickery' he needs to pay for
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns perspective - genuine human emotion as manipulation technique requiring professional consultation

jokes.text: I hate that fish!
jokes.setup: Discussion of the three-eyed fish political issue
jokes.punchline: Burns' petulant, personal animosity toward inanimate fish
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' childish blame of external factors rather than accepting responsibility

jokes.text: Oh, no! An election? That's when they close the bars, isn't it?
jokes.setup: TV announcement about upcoming election
jokes.punchline: Barney's only concern is bar closures on election day
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney character moment - reducing civic duty to personal inconvenience about drinking

jokes.text: I bet before the papers blew this out of proportion, you didn't even know how many eyes a fish had
jokes.setup: Marge concerned about three-eyed fish
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious defense missing the entire point of the mutation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - deflecting legitimate concern by questioning basic knowledge rather than addressing the issue

jokes.text: When this announcement's done, every Johnny Lunch-pail in this state will be eating out of my hand
jokes.setup: Burns confident about his political ad
jokes.punchline: Using working-class nickname while claiming they'll be subservient
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' condescension masked as folksy appeal to common voters

jokes.text: Actor portraying Charles Darwin
jokes.setup: Burns' political ad needs scientific authority
jokes.punchline: Blatantly fake expert with transparent disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of political ad manipulation - openly fake but presented as authoritative

jokes.text: It may be a kind of super-fish. I wouldn't mind a third eye, would you?
jokes.setup: Fake Darwin explaining mutation benefits
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting nuclear mutation as desirable human trait
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - normalizing radiation mutation through casual personal preference

jokes.text: They'd probably blame his ridiculous nose on the nuclear bogeyman
jokes.setup: Burns' ad comparing three-eyed fish to elephant
jokes.punchline: Calling elephant's trunk 'ridiculous nose' to normalize fish mutation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist comparison - treating natural evolution and radiation mutation as equivalent

jokes.text: Only a moron wouldn't cast his vote For Monty Burns
jokes.setup: Political ad jingle
jokes.punchline: Calling voters morons for not supporting Burns
jokes.explanation: Solid political satire - insulting potential voters in campaign materials

jokes.text: Wow! Super-fish! / I wish the government would get off his back / That Burns is just what this state needs, young blood!
jokes.setup: Bar patrons reacting to Burns political ad
jokes.punchline: Multiple characters completely buying the obvious propaganda
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing how easily manipulated the public can be by political advertising

jokes.text: Congratulations, Mr. Burns, the polls show you're up six points. / Giving me a total of? / Six
jokes.setup: Burns celebrating poll improvement
jokes.punchline: Revealing he still has only 6% total support
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - setup implies success but reveals pathetically low support level

jokes.text: And? / He felt her up. / Not good enough!
jokes.setup: Opposition research on Mary Bailey
jokes.punchline: Minor teenage indiscretion dismissed as insufficient dirt
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about desperate opposition research and impossibly high scandal standards

jokes.text: The voters see you as godlike. / Hot dog!
jokes.setup: Advisor's assessment of Burns' image
jokes.punchline: Burns' childlike excitement at being called godlike
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Burns' megalomaniacal satisfaction expressed in surprisingly folksy terms

jokes.text: Every Joe Meatball and Sally Housecoat in this godforsaken state will see me chow down with Eddie Punchclock
jokes.setup: Burns planning to eat with common worker
jokes.punchline: His condescending made-up names for working-class people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Burns' complete disconnection from regular people expressed through hilariously patronizing nicknames

jokes.text: Can you memorize this? 'Your campaign's moving like a runaway freight train. Why are you so popular?'
jokes.setup: Child needs to ask Burns a question
jokes.punchline: Obviously scripted, leading question designed to make Burns look good
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about staged media events and planted questions

jokes.text: Can I ask him to assuage my fears that he's contaminating the planet and making it uninhabitable?
jokes.setup: Lisa offered chance to ask Burns a question
jokes.punchline: Her sophisticated, accusatory question contrasting with scripted softball
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa characterization - using advanced vocabulary to ask genuinely important question that adults avoid

jokes.text: Finally, Mr. Burns wants you to appear very affectionate to him, but remember, he hates being touched
jokes.setup: Instructions for family dinner appearance
jokes.punchline: Contradictory requirements revealing Burns' fundamental inhumanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - needing to appear human while being fundamentally repulsed by human contact

jokes.text: You don't let me express myself. / You express yourself in the home you keep and the food you serve
jokes.setup: Marge wants to express herself politically
jokes.punchline: Homer's casually sexist dismissal limiting her to domestic sphere
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's unconscious sexism through seemingly reasonable statement

jokes.text: We're eating with the common man, not Tyrone Power.
jokes.setup: Burns looks too polished for common man dinner
jokes.punchline: Reference to glamorous 1940s movie star
jokes.explanation: Decent reference humor. Shows campaign's awareness of Burns' artificiality.

jokes.text: And look, I've brought noodle kugel.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to appear folksy and relatable
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at ethnic food familiarity
jokes.explanation: Burns trying to connect culturally but clearly out of his element. Forced relatability.

jokes.text: I love dogs. Babies too.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to recover from dog attack
jokes.punchline: His awkward, calculated declaration of loving things he clearly doesn't
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - transparent attempt to appear human through obviously scripted emotional claims

jokes.text: God, we paid for all this ourselves, so thanks for nothing
jokes.setup: Bart asked to say grace before dinner
jokes.punchline: His irreverent, logical dismissal of thanking God for self-purchased food
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart moment - combining his rebelliousness with surprisingly logical theological argument

jokes.text: Only a child could get away with such blasphemy. God bless them all. Amen.
jokes.setup: Bart's irreverent grace
jokes.punchline: Burns' calculated political spin turning blasphemy into charming innocence
jokes.explanation: Good Burns political maneuvering - turning potential disaster into folksy charm through spin

jokes.text: Oh, Mom, that felt awful. / I'm sorry. It will all be over soon. / But we've become the tools of evil
jokes.setup: Lisa forced to ask scripted question
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic realization about participating in political manipulation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa characterization - sophisticated moral awareness of being used for political purposes

jokes.text: I don't believe it! / He's blown it! / Ruined before it hit the ground
jokes.setup: Burns unable to swallow the three-eyed fish on camera
jokes.punchline: Campaign team's immediate recognition of political disaster
jokes.explanation: Good political satire - single moment destroying entire campaign through authentic human reaction

jokes.text: Here's your headline, 'Burns Can't Swallow Own Story'
jokes.setup: Burns' failure to eat the fish on camera
jokes.punchline: Perfect headline combining literal and metaphorical meanings of 'swallow'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - double meaning perfectly captures both literal event and political implications with maximum economy

jokes.text: You can't do this to me! I'm Charles Montgomery Burns!
jokes.setup: Campaign team abandoning Burns after fish disaster
jokes.punchline: Burns' imperious outrage invoking his full name as if it grants immunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - aristocratic entitlement in face of deserved consequences

jokes.text: I hardly see what destroying our meager possessions will accomplish
jokes.setup: Burns ordering destruction of Simpson house
jokes.punchline: Smithers' reasonable objection to pointless vandalism
jokes.explanation: Good Smithers character moment - being voice of reason even in Burns' vengeful tantrum

jokes.text: She's right. Take me home, Smithers. We'll destroy something tasteful
jokes.setup: Marge's reasonable objection to destroying their house
jokes.punchline: Burns redirecting destructive impulse toward expensive items
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns character moment - redirecting rage toward more expensive targets reveals his priorities perfectly

jokes.text: This anonymous clan of slack-jawed troglodytes cost me the election, but if I had them killed, I'd be the one to go to jail. That's democracy
jokes.setup: Burns reflecting on his election loss
jokes.punchline: Casually considering murder while lamenting democratic constraints
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns moment - combining elaborate insults, casual evil, and twisted understanding of democracy in one perfect line

jokes.text: Simpson, I shall make it the focus of my remaining years that your dreams will go unfulfilled
jokes.setup: Burns blaming Homer for election loss
jokes.punchline: His melodramatic vow of eternal vengeance over minor slight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns villainy - operatic revenge threats over petty political embarrassment

jokes.text: When a man's biggest dreams include seconds on dessert, occasional snuggling and sleeping in on weekends, no one man can destroy them
jokes.setup: Homer worried about Burns destroying his dreams
jokes.punchline: Marge's sweet observation that Homer's dreams are too simple to destroy
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Marge's loving understanding of Homer's simple pleasures as both endearing and indestructible

tmdb_overview: Bart catches a three-eyed fish in a river downstream of the Nuclear Power Plant. This causes the plant to become inspected, and in order to prevent it from being shut down, Mr. Burns decides to run for governor. After a hard campaign which sees Burns rise from being universally despised to running neck and neck with popular incumbent Mary Bailey, it is decided that Burns will have dinner with a random employee the night before the election. Homer is chosen, much to Marge's chagrin.
insight: Political satire peaks at 84.5 with a mutant fish joke anchoring the entire plot.
